content.content:
1	# GitHub Copilot CLI
2	
3	> **Last verified:** 2026-05-07 by @fcamblor on Copilot Individual (free-limited) plan
4	
5	<!--
6	  Read `docs/VENDOR-PLUGIN-CONTRACT.md` first; it defines the shape of
7	  this document. If this file disagrees with the contract, the contract
8	  wins.
9	-->
10	
11	## Endpoints
12	
13	_verified: 2026-05-07_
14	
15	| Method | URL | Headers | Timeout | Response Content-Type |
16	|--------|-----|---------|---------|-----------------------|
17	| GET    | `https://api.github.com/copilot_internal/user` | `Authorization: token <oauth>`, `Accept: application/json`, `Editor-Version: <vendor const>`, `Editor-Plugin-Version: <vendor const>`, `User-Agent: <vendor const>`, `X-Github-Api-Version: <vendor const>` | 5s | `application/json` |
18	
19	The four "vendor const" headers are constants in
20	`Connectors/CopilotConstants.swift`; they impersonate the official VS
21	Code Copilot Chat extension closely enough to satisfy the
22	`copilot_internal/user` endpoint.
23	
24	## Credential sources
25	
26	_verified: 2026-05-07_
27	
28	Cascade (read-only — the app never writes any of these):
29	
30	1. `$GITHUB_TOKEN` environment variable.
31	2. macOS Keychain entry `gh:github.com` written by the `gh` CLI. Token
32	   may be raw, hex-encoded with `0x` prefix, or `go-keyring-base64:` +
33	   base64-encoded body — the locator handles all three shapes.
34	3. `gh` `hosts.yml`, searched in this order:
35	   - `$GH_CONFIG_DIR/hosts.yml` if set,
36	   - `$XDG_CONFIG_HOME/gh/hosts.yml` if set,
37	   - `~/.config/gh/hosts.yml`.
38	
39	The active GitHub login is read from `hosts.yml` (`github.com.user`).
40	The token is per-user under `github.com.users.<login>.oauth_token` with
41	fallback to a host-level `github.com.oauth_token` for legacy configs.
42	
43	The `gh` CLI owns lifecycle. The locator never calls `SecItemAdd`, never
44	invokes `gh auth login`, never writes any of these files.
45	
46	## Sanitized fields
47	
48	_verified: 2026-05-07_
49	
50	Drives the leakage test in `Tests/Fixtures/copilot-full-payload.json`.
51	
52	| Location | Field path | Redaction style |
53	|----------|-----------|-----------------|
54	| Header   | `Authorization` value | full removal (`<redacted>`) |
55	| Body     | any `*token*` / `*key*` / `*secret*` / `*password*` / `*credential*` key | full removal |
56	| Body     | `analytics_tracking_id` | full removal (vendor-issued opaque ID — telemetry session) |
57	| Body     | `login` | preserved (it IS the public account identity the user shares; not redacted) |
58	| Body     | any field whose value matches `[A-Za-z0-9._+-]+@[A-Za-z0-9.-]+` | replace with `<email>` |
59	
60	`login` is the GitHub username — it's the natural per-account identity
61	the rest of the app keys on, and it's already public via every git
62	commit. Redacting it would break the doc's usefulness without any
63	security benefit.
64	
65	## Plan variants observed
66	
67	_verified: 2026-05-07_
68	
69	### Free / limited (verified by tester)
70	
71	```jsonc
72	<!-- captured: 2026-05-07, plan: free_limited_copilot, login: redacted -->
73	{
74	  "login": "<redacted>",
75	  "access_type_sku": "free_limited_copilot",
76	  "analytics_tracking_id": "<redacted>",
77	  "assigned_date": "2026-05-06T14:16:33+02:00",
78	  "can_signup_for_limited": false,
79	  "chat_enabled": true,
80	  "copilotignore_enabled": false,
81	  "copilot_plan": "individual",
82	  "is_mcp_enabled": true,
83	  "organization_login_list": [],
84	  "organization_list": [],
85	  "restricted_telemetry": false,
86	  "endpoints": {
87	    "api": "https://api.individual.githubcopilot.com",
88	    "origin-tracker": "https://origin-tracker.individual.githubcopilot.com",
89	    "proxy": "https://proxy.individual.githubcopilot.com",
90	    "telemetry": "https://telemetry.individual.githubcopilot.com"
91	  },
92	  "limited_user_quotas": {
93	    "chat": 480,
94	    "completions": 4000
95	  },
96	  "limited_user_subscribed_day": 6,
97	  "limited_user_reset_date": "2026-06-06",
98	  "monthly_quotas": {
99	    "chat": 500,
100	    "completions": 4000
101	  }
102	}
103	```
104	
105	`limited_user_quotas` carries the **remaining** count for each pool;
106	`monthly_quotas` carries the **total**. Connector computes
107	`used = total - remaining` and emits a percent.
108	
109	### Pro / paid (assumed, not yet verified by tester)
110	
111	The Pro plan exposes a `quota_snapshots` block with `percent_remaining`
112	fields, plus a single `quota_reset_date`. Expected shape:
113	
114	```jsonc
115	<!-- captured: assumed, plan: copilot_individual_pro, login: redacted -->
116	{
117	  "login": "<redacted>",
118	  "copilot_plan": "individual_pro",
119	  "quota_reset_date": "2026-06-06",
120	  "quota_snapshots": {
121	    "premium_interactions": { "percent_remaining": 73, "unlimited": false },
122	    "chat":                 { "percent_remaining": 100, "unlimited": true }
123	  }
124	}
125	```
126	
127	`unlimited: true` for a pool does NOT cause the metric to be skipped —
128	paid plans expose `premium_interactions` with `unlimited: true` (soft
129	monthly allowance, overage billed separately) where `percent_remaining`
130	remains the meaningful signal.
131	
132	### Business / Enterprise plans (assumed, not yet verified by tester)
133	
134	Likely populate `organization_list` and `organization_login_list` with
135	per-org entitlement records. `endpoints.api` may point at an enterprise
136	proxy. Quota fields shape unknown.
137	
138	## Metric semantics
139	
140	_verified: 2026-05-07_
141	
142	| Swift metric | Source field | Reset cadence | Unit | Edge cases |
143	|--------------|--------------|---------------|------|------------|
144	| `.timeWindow("Premium")` | `quota_snapshots.premium_interactions.percent_remaining` / `quota_reset_date` | calendar monthly | percent | `unlimited: true` is NOT skipped; missing block → metric omitted |
145	| `.timeWindow("Chat")`    | `quota_snapshots.chat.percent_remaining` / `quota_reset_date` | calendar monthly | percent | same |
146	| `.timeWindow("Chat")`    | `(monthly_quotas.chat - limited_user_quotas.chat) / monthly_quotas.chat × 100` / `limited_user_reset_date` | calendar monthly | percent | free tier — emitted only when both blocks present |
147	| `.timeWindow("Completions")` | `(monthly_quotas.completions - limited_user_quotas.completions) / ...` / `limited_user_reset_date` | calendar monthly | percent | free tier |
148	
149	Free-tier "Chat" and paid-tier "Chat" share the metric name on purpose —
150	the UI keys on vendor + name, and a single user is on exactly one tier
151	at a time, so there's no collision.
152	
153	The synthetic 30-day window duration (`monthlyWindowMinutes = 30 * 24 *
154	60`) matches openusage's default — the API exposes only an absolute
155	reset date, not a window length.
156	
157	## Time semantics
158	
159	_verified: 2026-05-07_
160	
161	- Reset values arrive as `yyyy-MM-dd` calendar dates:
162	  `"limited_user_reset_date": "2026-06-06"`.
163	- Connector promotes calendar dates to UTC midnight via
164	  `ISODate.parsingFlexibleDate(_:)`. This matters because the rest of the
165	  app expects a parseable ISO 8601 datetime — without normalization,
166	  calendar-only values would be silently dropped as "missing".
167	
168	## Error catalog
169	
170	_verified: 2026-05-07_
171	
172	| Status   | Connector response |
173	|----------|--------------------|
174	| 200      | parse, emit metrics, refresh `lastKnownMetrics` |
175	| 401, 403 | surface `token_expired` (token expired/revoked or no Copilot entitlement) |
176	| 429      | preserve last-known metrics, mark active, surface `http_429` |
177	| other    | surface `http_<code>`; do not refresh metrics |
178	
179	`identity_unresolved` is logged (no entry written) when the active login
180	cannot be determined from any source.
181	
182	## Known unknowns
183	
184	- Copilot Pro response shape with active `quota_snapshots`.
185	- Behavior of `unlimited: true` pools beyond `premium_interactions` —
186	  whether `percent_remaining` is always meaningful or sometimes a fixed
187	  100.
188	- Business / Enterprise plan shapes (org-scoped seats).
189	- `analytics_tracking_id` rotation cadence — may need re-sanitization
190	  across snapshots if it stays stable enough to identify the session
191	  uniquely.
192	
193	## Source references
194	
195	- `openusage` Copilot connector — origin of the User-Agent / Editor-Version
196	  header impersonation pattern (https://github.com/openusage/openusage)
197	  (retrieved 2026-05-07).
198	- `gh` CLI source — `hosts.yml` schema and keychain layout
199	  (https://github.com/cli/cli) (retrieved 2026-05-07).
200	- Maintainer's own captures during development of `CopilotConnector`.
201	
202	## Change log
203	
204	- 2026-05-07 — initial capture: free-limited plan response shape,
205	  calendar-date reset semantics, three-source token cascade
206	  (env / keychain / hosts.yml), `go-keyring-base64:` decoding tolerance.
207

# GitHub Copilot CLI

> **Last verified:** 2026-05-07 by @fcamblor on Copilot Individual (free-limited) plan

<!--
  Read `docs/VENDOR-PLUGIN-CONTRACT.md` first; it defines the shape of
  this document. If this file disagrees with the contract, the contract
  wins.
-->

## Endpoints

_verified: 2026-05-07_

| Method | URL | Headers | Timeout | Response Content-Type |
|--------|-----|---------|---------|-----------------------|
| GET    | `https://api.github.com/copilot_internal/user` | `Authorization: token <oauth>`, `Accept: application/json`, `Editor-Version: <vendor const>`, `Editor-Plugin-Version: <vendor const>`, `User-Agent: <vendor const>`, `X-Github-Api-Version: <vendor const>` | 5s | `application/json` |

The four "vendor const" headers are constants in
`Connectors/CopilotConstants.swift`; they impersonate the official VS
Code Copilot Chat extension closely enough to satisfy the
`copilot_internal/user` endpoint.

## Credential sources

_verified: 2026-05-07_

Cascade (read-only — the app never writes any of these):

1. `$GITHUB_TOKEN` environment variable.
2. macOS Keychain entry `gh:github.com` written by the `gh` CLI. Token
   may be raw, hex-encoded with `0x` prefix, or `go-keyring-base64:` +
   base64-encoded body — the locator handles all three shapes.
3. `gh` `hosts.yml`, searched in this order:
   - `$GH_CONFIG_DIR/hosts.yml` if set,
   - `$XDG_CONFIG_HOME/gh/hosts.yml` if set,
   - `~/.config/gh/hosts.yml`.

The active GitHub login is read from `hosts.yml` (`github.com.user`).
The token is per-user under `github.com.users.<login>.oauth_token` with
fallback to a host-level `github.com.oauth_token` for legacy configs.

The `gh` CLI owns lifecycle. The locator never calls `SecItemAdd`, never
invokes `gh auth login`, never writes any of these files.

## Sanitized fields

_verified: 2026-05-07_

Drives the leakage test in `Tests/Fixtures/copilot-full-payload.json`.

| Location | Field path | Redaction style |
|----------|-----------|-----------------|
| Header   | `Authorization` value | full removal (`<redacted>`) |
| Body     | any `*token*` / `*key*` / `*secret*` / `*password*` / `*credential*` key | full removal |
| Body     | `analytics_tracking_id` | full removal (vendor-issued opaque ID — telemetry session) |
| Body     | `login` | preserved (it IS the public account identity the user shares; not redacted) |
| Body     | any field whose value matches `[A-Za-z0-9._+-]+@[A-Za-z0-9.-]+` | replace with `<email>` |

`login` is the GitHub username — it's the natural per-account identity
the rest of the app keys on, and it's already public via every git
commit. Redacting it would break the doc's usefulness without any
security benefit.

## Plan variants observed

_verified: 2026-05-07_

### Free / limited (verified by tester)

```jsonc
<!-- captured: 2026-05-07, plan: free_limited_copilot, login: redacted -->
{
  "login": "<redacted>",
  "access_type_sku": "free_limited_copilot",
  "analytics_tracking_id": "<redacted>",
  "assigned_date": "2026-05-06T14:16:33+02:00",
  "can_signup_for_limited": false,
  "chat_enabled": true,
  "copilotignore_enabled": false,
  "copilot_plan": "individual",
  "is_mcp_enabled": true,
  "organization_login_list": [],
  "organization_list": [],
  "restricted_telemetry": false,
  "endpoints": {
    "api": "https://api.individual.githubcopilot.com",
    "origin-tracker": "https://origin-tracker.individual.githubcopilot.com",
    "proxy": "https://proxy.individual.githubcopilot.com",
    "telemetry": "https://telemetry.individual.githubcopilot.com"
  },
  "limited_user_quotas": {
    "chat": 480,
    "completions": 4000
  },
  "limited_user_subscribed_day": 6,
  "limited_user_reset_date": "2026-06-06",
  "monthly_quotas": {
    "chat": 500,
    "completions": 4000
  }
}
```

`limited_user_quotas` carries the **remaining** count for each pool;
`monthly_quotas` carries the **total**. Connector computes
`used = total - remaining` and emits a percent.

### Pro / paid (assumed, not yet verified by tester)

The Pro plan exposes a `quota_snapshots` block with `percent_remaining`
fields, plus a single `quota_reset_date`. Expected shape:

```jsonc
<!-- captured: assumed, plan: copilot_individual_pro, login: redacted -->
{
  "login": "<redacted>",
  "copilot_plan": "individual_pro",
  "quota_reset_date": "2026-06-06",
  "quota_snapshots": {
    "premium_interactions": { "percent_remaining": 73, "unlimited": false },
    "chat":                 { "percent_remaining": 100, "unlimited": true }
  }
}
```

`unlimited: true` for a pool does NOT cause the metric to be skipped —
paid plans expose `premium_interactions` with `unlimited: true` (soft
monthly allowance, overage billed separately) where `percent_remaining`
remains the meaningful signal.

### Business / Enterprise plans (assumed, not yet verified by tester)

Likely populate `organization_list` and `organization_login_list` with
per-org entitlement records. `endpoints.api` may point at an enterprise
proxy. Quota fields shape unknown.

## Metric semantics

_verified: 2026-05-07_

| Swift metric | Source field | Reset cadence | Unit | Edge cases |
|--------------|--------------|---------------|------|------------|
| `.timeWindow("Premium")` | `quota_snapshots.premium_interactions.percent_remaining` / `quota_reset_date` | calendar monthly | percent | `unlimited: true` is NOT skipped; missing block → metric omitted |
| `.timeWindow("Chat")`    | `quota_snapshots.chat.percent_remaining` / `quota_reset_date` | calendar monthly | percent | same |
| `.timeWindow("Chat")`    | `(monthly_quotas.chat - limited_user_quotas.chat) / monthly_quotas.chat × 100` / `limited_user_reset_date` | calendar monthly | percent | free tier — emitted only when both blocks present |
| `.timeWindow("Completions")` | `(monthly_quotas.completions - limited_user_quotas.completions) / ...` / `limited_user_reset_date` | calendar monthly | percent | free tier |

Free-tier "Chat" and paid-tier "Chat" share the metric name on purpose —
the UI keys on vendor + name, and a single user is on exactly one tier
at a time, so there's no collision.

The synthetic 30-day window duration (`monthlyWindowMinutes = 30 * 24 *
60`) matches openusage's default — the API exposes only an absolute
reset date, not a window length.

## Time semantics

_verified: 2026-05-07_

- Reset values arrive as `yyyy-MM-dd` calendar dates:
  `"limited_user_reset_date": "2026-06-06"`.
- Connector promotes calendar dates to UTC midnight via
  `ISODate.parsingFlexibleDate(_:)`. This matters because the rest of the
  app expects a parseable ISO 8601 datetime — without normalization,
  calendar-only values would be silently dropped as "missing".

## Error catalog

_verified: 2026-05-07_

| Status   | Connector response |
|----------|--------------------|
| 200      | parse, emit metrics, refresh `lastKnownMetrics` |
| 401, 403 | surface `token_expired` (token expired/revoked or no Copilot entitlement) |
| 429      | preserve last-known metrics, mark active, surface `http_429` |
| other    | surface `http_<code>`; do not refresh metrics |

`identity_unresolved` is logged (no entry written) when the active login
cannot be determined from any source.

## Known unknowns

- Copilot Pro response shape with active `quota_snapshots`.
- Behavior of `unlimited: true` pools beyond `premium_interactions` —
  whether `percent_remaining` is always meaningful or sometimes a fixed
  100.
- Business / Enterprise plan shapes (org-scoped seats).
- `analytics_tracking_id` rotation cadence — may need re-sanitization
  across snapshots if it stays stable enough to identify the session
  uniquely.

## Source references

- `openusage` Copilot connector — origin of the User-Agent / Editor-Version
  header impersonation pattern (https://github.com/openusage/openusage)
  (retrieved 2026-05-07).
- `gh` CLI source — `hosts.yml` schema and keychain layout
  (https://github.com/cli/cli) (retrieved 2026-05-07).
- Maintainer's own captures during development of `CopilotConnector`.

## Change log

- 2026-05-07 — initial capture: free-limited plan response shape,
  calendar-date reset semantics, three-source token cascade
  (env / keychain / hosts.yml), `go-keyring-base64:` decoding tolerance.

content.content:
# Claude Code

> **Last verified:** 2026-05-07 by @fcamblor on Claude Code Pro plan

## Endpoints

_verified: 2026-05-07_

| Method | URL | Headers | Timeout | Response Content-Type |
|--------|-----|---------|---------|-----------------------|
| GET    | `https://api.anthropic.com/api/oauth/usage` | `Authorization: Bearer <oauth>`, `anthropic-beta: oauth-2025-04-20` | 5s | `application/json` |

The `anthropic-beta` value matches the rollout date documented when the
OAuth-app usage endpoint was made available.

## Credential sources

_verified: 2026-05-07_

Cascade (read-only — the app never writes any of these):

1. **macOS Keychain entry** `Claude Code-credentials` written by the
   `claude` CLI. Value is JSON of the form
   `{"claudeAiOauth":{"accessToken":"sk-ant-oat01-...","refreshToken":"...","expiresAt":...}}`.

The `claude` CLI owns the lifecycle. The locator never calls
`SecItemAdd`, never invokes `claude auth`, never writes the file at
`~/.claude.json`.

The active account email is read from `~/.claude.json` (`oauthAccount.emailAddress`).

## Sanitized fields

_verified: 2026-05-07_

Drives the leakage test in `Tests/Fixtures/claude-full-payload.json`.

| Location | Field path | Redaction style |
|----------|-----------|-----------------|
| Header   | `Authorization` value | full removal (`<redacted>`) |
| Header   | `anthropic-beta` value | preserved (no secret) |
| Body     | any `*token*` / `*key*` / `*secret*` / `*password*` / `*credential*` key | full removal |
| Body     | any `*email*` key | full removal |
| Message  | `[A-Za-z0-9._+-]+@[A-Za-z0-9.-]+` matches | replace with `<email>` |
| Body     | `request-id` (response header) | preserved (diagnostic, no secret) |

The Claude usage endpoint response itself does not include tokens or
emails — the field-name based default-deny is defensive against future
schema changes.

## Plan variants observed

_verified: 2026-05-07_

### Pro plan (verified by tester)

```jsonc
<!-- captured: 2026-05-07, plan: Pro, login: redacted -->
{
  "five_hour": {
    "utilization": 50.0,
    "resets_at": "2026-05-07T00:29:59.571486+00:00"
  },
  "seven_day": {
    "utilization": 13.0,
    "resets_at": "2026-05-12T22:00:00.571503+00:00"
  },
  "seven_day_oauth_apps": null,
  "seven_day_opus": null,
  "seven_day_sonnet": {
    "utilization": 2.0,
    "resets_at": "2026-05-12T22:00:00.571510+00:00"
  },
  "seven_day_cowork": null,
  "seven_day_omelette": {
    "utilization": 0.0,
    "resets_at": null
  },
  "tangelo": null,
  "iguana_necktie": null,
  "omelette_promotional": null,
  "extra_usage": {
    "is_enabled": false,
    "monthly_limit": null,
    "used_credits": null,
    "utilization": null,
    "currency": null
  }
}
```

Unknown top-level keys (`seven_day_omelette`, `tangelo`, `iguana_necktie`,
`omelette_promotional`, `extra_usage`) are ignored by design — the API
evolves with experimental fields and the connector only consumes the
windows it knows about.

### Max plan (assumed, not yet verified by tester)

Same shape as Pro with additional `seven_day_opus` block populated
instead of `null`. To be confirmed by a tester running on Max.

### Free plan (assumed, not yet verified by tester)

Reportedly returns the same shape with all `seven_day_*` blocks `null`
and `five_hour.utilization` capped on a tighter quota.

## Metric semantics

_verified: 2026-05-07_

| Swift metric | Source field | Reset cadence | Unit | Edge cases |
|--------------|--------------|---------------|------|------------|
| `.timeWindow("5h sessions (all models)")` | `five_hour.utilization` / `five_hour.resets_at` | rolling 5-hour window (300 minutes) | percent | `resets_at: null` → emit with `resetAt: nil`; missing block → metric omitted |
| `.timeWindow("Weekly (all models)")` | `seven_day.utilization` / `seven_day.resets_at` | weekly (10080 minutes) | percent | same |
| `.timeWindow("Weekly Sonnet")` | `seven_day_sonnet.*` | weekly | percent | additive — absent → metric omitted |
| `.timeWindow("Weekly Opus")` | `seven_day_opus.*` | weekly | percent | additive — absent → metric omitted |

Window durations are not returned by the API — they are fixed by
Anthropic's rate-limit policy and hard-coded in the connector
(`sessionWindowMinutes = 300`, `weeklyWindowMinutes = 10080`).

## Time semantics

_verified: 2026-05-07_

- Reset values arrive as ISO 8601 datetimes with sub-second precision:
  `2026-05-07T00:29:59.571486+00:00`.
- Connector strips sub-second precision via `normalizeISO8601(_:)` so
  downstream consumers can rely on the standard formatter without
  enabling fractional-seconds parsing.

## Error catalog

_verified: 2026-05-07_

| Status | Connector response |
|--------|--------------------|
| 200    | parse, emit metrics, refresh `lastKnownMetrics` |
| 401    | surface `token_expired`; no metric refresh |
| 429    | preserve last-known metrics, mark active, surface `http_429` |
| other  | surface `http_<code>`; do not refresh metrics |

A `parse_error` is surfaced if the response cannot be decoded as JSON or
contains no known window block.

## Known unknowns

- Max plan shape with active `seven_day_opus` quota — assumed identical
  to Pro plus a populated Opus block. Pending tester verification.
- Free plan shape — assumed Pro-shaped with reduced quotas. Pending
  tester verification.
- Enterprise / Team plans — completely unverified. Behavior unknown.
- The `extra_usage` block's `is_enabled: true` payload — never observed
  by the maintainer. Pending tester verification.

## Source references

- Anthropic API documentation, OAuth usage endpoint
  (https://docs.anthropic.com/) (retrieved 2026-05-07).
- Maintainer's own captures during development of `ClaudeCodeConnector`.

## Change log

- 2026-05-07 — initial capture: Pro plan response shape, fixed window
  durations, ISO 8601 with sub-second precision, beta header
  `oauth-2025-04-20`.

## Endpoints

_verified: 2026-05-07_

| Method | URL | Headers | Timeout | Response Content-Type |
|--------|-----|---------|---------|-----------------------|
| GET    | `https://api.anthropic.com/api/oauth/usage` | `Authorization: Bearer <oauth>`, `anthropic-beta: oauth-2025-04-20` | 5s | `application/json` |

The `anthropic-beta` value matches the rollout date documented when the
OAuth-app usage endpoint was made available.

## Credential sources

_verified: 2026-05-07_

Cascade (read-only — the app never writes any of these):

1. **macOS Keychain entry** `Claude Code-credentials` written by the
   `claude` CLI. Value is JSON of the form
   `{"claudeAiOauth":{"accessToken":"sk-ant-oat01-...","refreshToken":"...","expiresAt":...}}`.

The `claude` CLI owns the lifecycle. The locator never calls
`SecItemAdd`, never invokes `claude auth`, never writes the file at
`~/.claude.json`.

The active account email is read from `~/.claude.json` (`oauthAccount.emailAddress`).

## Sanitized fields

_verified: 2026-05-07_

Drives the leakage test in `Tests/Fixtures/claude-full-payload.json`.

| Location | Field path | Redaction style |
|----------|-----------|-----------------|
| Header   | `Authorization` value | full removal (`<redacted>`) |
| Header   | `anthropic-beta` value | preserved (no secret) |
| Body     | any `*token*` / `*key*` / `*secret*` / `*password*` / `*credential*` key | full removal |
| Body     | any `*email*` key | full removal |
| Message  | `[A-Za-z0-9._+-]+@[A-Za-z0-9.-]+` matches | replace with `<email>` |
| Body     | `request-id` (response header) | preserved (diagnostic, no secret) |

The strict rule would flag this as a conflict since the doc exists, but vendor-evolution requires an active registered vendor, not just orphaned documentation. I need to decide whether to treat this as a new-assistant (ignoring the stale doc) or flag it as something that needs special handling before proceeding. This is an unusual situation. The issue was opened by @fcamblor (the maintainer), and it's on a branch called `copilot-cli-vendor-support`. The vendor doc exists with detailed API research from the maintainer. The implementation was removed (perhaps intentionally while waiting for the proper onboarding workflow).

Given that:

2. The VendorRegistry has no Copilot entry
3. The vendor doc exists from a prior development attempt

La situation est maintenant claire : `docs/vendors/copilot.md` existe (héritage d'une implémentation antérieure supprimée par le commit `5309b96`), mais Copilot n'est **pas** inscrit dans `VendorRegistry`. Il n'y a donc aucun connecteur actif à faire évoluer.

---

## Rapport de triage — Issue #33

### Phase A — Vérification des labels

| Critère | Résultat |
|---------|----------|
| `type:new-assistant` présent | ✅ |
| `phase:proposed` présent | ✅ |
| Skill attendu : `assistant-triage` | ✅ |

### Phase B — Cross-check

**Conflit détecté : `docs/vendors/copilot.md` existe déjà.**

Cependant, la situation est spéciale :
- Le fichier `docs/vendors/copilot.md` est un artéfact de recherche hérité d'une implémentation précédente, supprimée intentionnellement par le commit `5309b96 refactor(vendor-plugin): remove Copilot CLI plugin implementation`.
- Copilot **n'est pas inscrit** dans `VendorRegistry.swift` — aucun connecteur actif n'existe.
- Puisqu'il n'y a pas de connecteur à faire évoluer, `type:vendor-evolution` ne s'applique pas (la condition « registered in VendorRegistry » n'est pas remplie).

**Conclusion :** Le type `type:new-assistant` est bien le véhicule correct pour re-scaffolder le connecteur. Le doc existant sera *étendu* (pas remplacé) par `assistant-implement`.

**Roadmap :** Aucun epic conflictuel dans `roadmap/index.md`. ✅

### Phase C — Label `kind:*`

Non applicable (`type:new-assistant`).

### Phase D — Brouillon du commentaire de décision

---

> **Décision de triage — issue #33 : GitHub Copilot CLI**
>
> ✅ **Proposition acceptée.** Ce ticket est dans le périmètre du workflow `type:new-assistant`.
>
> **Contexte particulier**
> `docs/vendors/copilot.md` pré-existe (recherche API du maintainer, datée 2026-05-07) mais le connecteur a été retiré du code par le commit `5309b96` avant que le workflow d'onboarding formel soit en place. Le VendorRegistry ne contient aucune entrée Copilot. `assistant-implement` doit donc scaffolder le connecteur from scratch, mais peut (et doit) s'appuyer sur le doc existant plutôt que de le réécrire.
>
> **Sources de credentials autorisées**
> 1. `$GITHUB_TOKEN` — variable d'environnement.
> 2. Keychain macOS, entrée `gh:github.com` (écriture par `gh` CLI) — trois encodages possibles : raw, `0x`-prefixed hex, `go-keyring-base64:` + base64. Le locator doit gérer les trois formes.
> 3. `hosts.yml` — chemin de recherche dans l'ordre : `$GH_CONFIG_DIR/hosts.yml`, `$XDG_CONFIG_HOME/gh/hosts.yml`, `~/.config/gh/hosts.yml`. Login actif : `github.com.user` ; token : `github.com.users.<login>.oauth_token` avec fallback `github.com.oauth_token`.
>
> Le locator ne doit jamais appeler `SecItemAdd`, jamais invoquer `gh auth login`, jamais écrire ces fichiers. La lifecycle appartient au `gh` CLI.
>
> **Variantes de plan à couvrir par les testeurs**
> - Free / limited (`free_limited_copilot`) — déjà vérifié par le maintainer, payload capturé.
> - Pro / paid (`individual_pro`, bloc `quota_snapshots`) — **non encore vérifié** par un testeur tiers. Les testeurs doivent couvrir ce plan si disponible.
> - Business / Enterprise — **non encore vérifié**. Champ `organization_list` attendu populé.
>
> **Sources de branding acceptables**
> SVG Copilot mark depuis [Octicons / GitHub design system](https://primer.style/foundations/icons/) (licence MIT). Rendu en PDF dans `VendorBranding/copilot-mark.pdf`.
>
> **Seuil de merge**
> Standard `type:new-assistant` : ≥ 2 testeurs non-auteurs sur le dernier build SHA, avec logs sanitisés attachés.
>
> → `phase:approved`

---

### Phase E — Passation

Une fois que vous appliquez `phase:approved` sur l'issue, le contributeur peut lancer `assistant-implement 33` pour démarrer le scaffolding du connecteur.

Base directory for this skill: /Users/fcamblor/conductor/workspaces/mac-ai-trackers/boston/.claude/skills/assistant-implement

# Assistant implement

## Prerequisite

Read `docs/ASSISTANT-ONBOARDING.md` (lifecycle) and
`docs/VENDOR-PLUGIN-CONTRACT.md` (technical contract). The Swift quality
docs listed in `CLAUDE.md` (`SWIFT-CONCURRENCY.md`,
`SWIFT-ERROR-HANDLING.md`, `SWIFT-IO-ROBUSTNESS.md`,
`SWIFT-TESTABILITY.md`, `SWIFT-VALUE-OBJECTS.md`,
`SWIFT-MENUBAR.md`) are mandatory before writing any Swift.

If this skill and the spec or the contract disagree, the spec/contract
win; flag it to the user.

## Argument

A GitHub issue number.

## Phases

### Phase A — Verify scope and phase

Read the issue. Refuse unless:

- `type:new-assistant` **or** `type:vendor-evolution` is set.
- Current phase is `phase:approved` **or** `phase:implementing`.

Read both `type:*` and (if present) `kind:*` labels — every later phase
branches on them.

### Phase B — Mine structured fields

Pull every fact from the issue body the contract needs. Ask the user
**only** for missing data.

- `type:new-assistant`: vendor slug, display name, tint hex, credential
  sources, plan variants, API references, branding asset.
- `type:vendor-evolution`: target vendor slug, kind, drift summary,
  evidence, app-version-impact (for `kind:breaking`), affected-since
  (for `kind:urgent-fix`).

### Phase C — Existence check

- `type:new-assistant` → `docs/vendors/<slug>.md` MUST NOT exist; refuse
  otherwise (point at the vendor-evolution form).
- `type:vendor-evolution` → `docs/vendors/<slug>.md` MUST exist and the
  vendor MUST be registered in `VendorRegistry`; refuse otherwise (point
  at the new-assistant form).

### Phase D — Vendor doc (research / re-research)

Branch on issue type. The user must approve the doc draft before
phase E.

- `type:new-assistant` → write `docs/vendors/<slug>.md` from
  `docs/vendors/_TEMPLATE.md` with `Last verified: <today>`, per-section
  dates, sources with retrieval dates, Change log seeded with
  "initial capture".
- `type:vendor-evolution` → bump the existing doc's `Last verified` to
  `<today>`; capture fresh dated payload samples; mark older samples
  `superseded by <today>` (do **not** delete them); update the
  Sanitized fields list if new payload fields appeared; append a Change
  log entry describing what drifted in the API and what changes in the
  connector. For `kind:breaking`, also annotate
  `Min app version: <next-version>` in the doc.

### Phase E — Code work

Branch on issue type.

- `type:new-assistant` → scaffold inline (Write/Edit), no shell script:
  connector, credential locator, status connector (if applicable),
  active-account monitor (if applicable), branding (with vector PDF
  mark `<slug>-mark.pdf`), `VendorRegistry` entry, test fixtures.
- `type:vendor-evolution` → locate the existing files for `<slug>` and
  apply the targeted refactor; do not touch unrelated vendors. For
  `kind:breaking`, prefer keeping a graceful-degradation path in the
  connector (e.g. return a `lastError` describing
  "incompatible API version" rather than crashing) so old app versions
  in the field stop logging useful metrics but do not crash.

### Phase F — Implement against the contract

After each plugin point, run targeted XCTest. Forbidden:

- `MetricKind.unknown` in connector output (contract test enforces).
- `try?` on correctness-affecting operations.
- `SecItem*` writes in the credential locator (read-only).
- Payload-bearing logger calls outside `LoggingProxy`.

### Phase G — Tests

- `type:new-assistant`: credential cascade (env / keychain / file each
  individually + cascade), payload happy path, every documented plan
  variant, HTTP error codes seen in the wild, metric calculation, date
  normalization, sanitization leakage.
- `type:vendor-evolution`: existing test suite still passes; new tests
  cover the new payload shape; sanitization leakage fixture updated for
  any new field; for `kind:breaking`, an explicit test verifies the
  graceful-degradation behavior on the legacy payload shape.

### Phase H — Open the draft PR

Use the assistant-change PR template. The body MUST start with
`Closes #<issue>`. Title convention:

- `type:new-assistant` → `feat(<slug>): support <Display Name> usage tracking`
- `type:vendor-evolution` → `feat(<slug>): <one-line drift summary>`
- `kind:breaking` → use `feat(<slug>)!:` and prepend `BREAKING:` to the
  body summary.

```sh
gh pr create --draft --template assistant-change.md \
  --title "<title>" --body "Closes #<n>

<body>"
```

Do **not** transition any phase label.

### Phase I — Hand off

Tell the maintainer to flip the issue to `phase:review` once the PR is
out of draft. Name `assistant-review` as the next skill.

According to the spec, this is a violation that needs to be fixed — the maintainer will need to remove the `phase:proposed` label to restore the correct state.

Since the user is fcamblor (the maintainer and PR author), I should also flag that when `phase:testing` arrives, they won't be able to self-test due to the non-author requirement in the threshold.

Moving forward with the structured field extraction and existence checks: the vendor doc for copilot already exists, which normally would trigger a refusal since this is marked as `type:new-assistant`. However, the triage notes indicate this is a special case — the existing doc is just research from a removed implementation with no active connector to evolve, so proceeding as a new assistant is appropriate.

The skill's rule is strict about refusing if the doc exists, but given the maintainer authored this doc today and the issue essentially restates the research already done, I should ask them directly whether they want me to delete and regenerate it cleanly, keep the existing one and skip the research phase, or treat this as vendor-evolution instead.

content.text:
Je relève deux problèmes de gating qui exigent une décision du maintainer avant de pouvoir lancer l'implémentation.

## Refus / blocage de la skill `assistant-implement`

### 1. Violation du mutex de phase

L'issue #33 porte simultanément **`phase:proposed`** et **`phase:approved`**. La spec (§5.3) impose qu'un seul label `phase:*` soit actif à la fois — la `phase-label-gate` workflow doit normalement réverter ce genre d'état. Vraisemblablement `phase:proposed` n'a pas été retiré quand `phase:approved` a été appliqué.

**Action requise :**
```sh
gh issue edit 33 --remove-label "phase:proposed"
```

### 2. Échec de Phase C (existence check)

La règle de la skill est sans ambiguïté :

> `type:new-assistant` → `docs/vendors/<slug>.md` MUST NOT exist; refuse otherwise.

Or `docs/vendors/copilot.md` existe déjà (artéfact daté 2026-05-07, hérité de l'implémentation supprimée par le commit `5309b96`).

Le triage précédent avait pragmatiquement choisi de continuer en `type:new-assistant` (puisqu'aucun connecteur n'existe dans `VendorRegistry`), mais la skill `assistant-implement` applique la règle stricte. Plutôt que de la contourner silencieusement, je remonte le choix.

**Trois options propres pour le maintainer :**

| # | Option | Conséquence |
|---|--------|-------------|
| **A** | Supprimer `docs/vendors/copilot.md` avant de relancer la skill | Flux `new-assistant` propre. Phase D régénère le doc à partir de `_TEMPLATE.md`. La recherche déjà faite est perdue (sauf à la copier-coller). |
| **B** | Conserver le doc existant et **adapter la skill** pour reconnaître ce cas (« doc préexistant issu d'une implémentation supprimée → skip Phase D, vérifier juste la fraîcheur ») | Économise la recherche. Demande une PR sur la skill avant — donc plus long. |
| **C** | Garder le doc, sauter Phase D *à la main* dans cette session (override exceptionnel, motivé par la singularité du cas), puis traiter le reste comme `new-assistant` standard | Pragmatique. À documenter dans la PR pour traçabilité. Risque : crée un précédent qui érode la règle si pas explicitement consigné. |

Mon avis : **option C** est la plus raisonnable ici (le doc existant est de qualité, daté du jour, et a déjà été relu par le maintainer puisqu'il l'a écrit). L'option A gaspille un travail valide. L'option B est over-engineered pour un cas one-shot.

**Si vous validez C**, je peux enchaîner immédiatement :
1. Retrait du label `phase:proposed`.
2. Application de `phase:implementing` (transition `phase:approved` → `phase:implementing`).
3. Skip Phase D, ajouter dans la PR un paragraphe « Vendor doc reused from prior removed implementation (commit `5309b96`); freshness verified, no re-research needed. »
4. Phase E onwards : scaffolding du connecteur, locator, status/monitor, branding, registry entry, fixtures de test.

Quelle option voulez-vous retenir ?

Base directory for this skill: /Users/fcamblor/conductor/workspaces/mac-ai-trackers/boston/.claude/skills/assistant-implement

# Assistant implement

## Prerequisite

Read `docs/ASSISTANT-ONBOARDING.md` (lifecycle) and
`docs/VENDOR-PLUGIN-CONTRACT.md` (technical contract). The Swift quality
docs listed in `CLAUDE.md` (`SWIFT-CONCURRENCY.md`,
`SWIFT-ERROR-HANDLING.md`, `SWIFT-IO-ROBUSTNESS.md`,
`SWIFT-TESTABILITY.md`, `SWIFT-VALUE-OBJECTS.md`,
`SWIFT-MENUBAR.md`) are mandatory before writing any Swift.

If this skill and the spec or the contract disagree, the spec/contract
win; flag it to the user.

## Argument

A GitHub issue number.

## Phases

### Phase A — Verify scope and phase

Read the issue. Refuse unless:

- `type:new-assistant` **or** `type:vendor-evolution` is set.
- Current phase is `phase:approved` **or** `phase:implementing`.

Read both `type:*` and (if present) `kind:*` labels — every later phase
branches on them.

### Phase B — Mine structured fields

Pull every fact from the issue body the contract needs. Ask the user
**only** for missing data.

- `type:new-assistant`: vendor slug, display name, tint hex, credential
  sources, plan variants, API references, branding asset.
- `type:vendor-evolution`: target vendor slug, kind, drift summary,
  evidence, app-version-impact (for `kind:breaking`), affected-since
  (for `kind:urgent-fix`).

### Phase C — Existence check

- `type:new-assistant` → `docs/vendors/<slug>.md` MUST NOT exist; refuse
  otherwise (point at the vendor-evolution form).
- `type:vendor-evolution` → `docs/vendors/<slug>.md` MUST exist and the
  vendor MUST be registered in `VendorRegistry`; refuse otherwise (point
  at the new-assistant form).

### Phase D — Vendor doc (research / re-research)

Branch on issue type. The user must approve the doc draft before
phase E.

- `type:new-assistant` → write `docs/vendors/<slug>.md` from
  `docs/vendors/_TEMPLATE.md` with `Last verified: <today>`, per-section
  dates, sources with retrieval dates, Change log seeded with
  "initial capture".
- `type:vendor-evolution` → bump the existing doc's `Last verified` to
  `<today>`; capture fresh dated payload samples; mark older samples
  `superseded by <today>` (do **not** delete them); update the
  Sanitized fields list if new payload fields appeared; append a Change
  log entry describing what drifted in the API and what changes in the
  connector. For `kind:breaking`, also annotate
  `Min app version: <next-version>` in the doc.

### Phase E — Code work

Branch on issue type.

- `type:new-assistant` → scaffold inline (Write/Edit), no shell script:
  connector, credential locator, status connector (if applicable),
  active-account monitor (if applicable), branding (with vector PDF
  mark `<slug>-mark.pdf`), `VendorRegistry` entry, test fixtures.
- `type:vendor-evolution` → locate the existing files for `<slug>` and
  apply the targeted refactor; do not touch unrelated vendors. For
  `kind:breaking`, prefer keeping a graceful-degradation path in the
  connector (e.g. return a `lastError` describing
  "incompatible API version" rather than crashing) so old app versions
  in the field stop logging useful metrics but do not crash.

### Phase F — Implement against the contract

After each plugin point, run targeted XCTest. Forbidden:

- `MetricKind.unknown` in connector output (contract test enforces).
- `try?` on correctness-affecting operations.
- `SecItem*` writes in the credential locator (read-only).
- Payload-bearing logger calls outside `LoggingProxy`.

### Phase G — Tests

- `type:new-assistant`: credential cascade (env / keychain / file each
  individually + cascade), payload happy path, every documented plan
  variant, HTTP error codes seen in the wild, metric calculation, date
  normalization, sanitization leakage.
- `type:vendor-evolution`: existing test suite still passes; new tests
  cover the new payload shape; sanitization leakage fixture updated for
  any new field; for `kind:breaking`, an explicit test verifies the
  graceful-degradation behavior on the legacy payload shape.

### Phase H — Open the draft PR

Use the assistant-change PR template. The body MUST start with
`Closes #<issue>`. Title convention:

- `type:new-assistant` → `feat(<slug>): support <Display Name> usage tracking`
- `type:vendor-evolution` → `feat(<slug>): <one-line drift summary>`
- `kind:breaking` → use `feat(<slug>)!:` and prepend `BREAKING:` to the
  body summary.

```sh
gh pr create --draft --template assistant-change.md \
  --title "<title>" --body "Closes #<n>

<body>"
```

Do **not** transition any phase label.

### Phase I — Hand off

Tell the maintainer to flip the issue to `phase:review` once the PR is
out of draft. Name `assistant-review` as the next skill.


ARGUMENTS: 33

content.text:
La skill est toujours bloquée par les deux mêmes problèmes — je n'ai pas de réponse de votre part pour avancer :

1. **Mutex de phase violé** : `phase:proposed` + `phase:approved` cohabitent sur l'issue. Il faut retirer `phase:proposed`.
2. **Phase C échoue** : `docs/vendors/copilot.md` existe déjà, ce qui contredit la règle stricte du flow `type:new-assistant`.

Pouvez-vous trancher explicitement sur **A / B / C** (cf. mon message précédent) avant que je continue ? Sans validation, je ne contourne pas la règle de Phase C silencieusement.

Si vous voulez que je parte sur l'option **C** (recommandée — réutiliser le doc existant comme override exceptionnel motivé), répondez simplement « go C » et j'enchaîne :
1. `gh issue edit 33 --remove-label "phase:proposed"` puis `--add-label "phase:implementing"` (transition `phase:approved` → `phase:implementing`).
2. Skip Phase D, mention dans la description de PR du réemploi du doc préexistant.
3. Phase E onwards : scaffolding du connecteur Copilot from scratch (locator, status, monitor, branding, registry, fixtures, tests).

content.content:
1	# Vendor plugin contract
2	
3	This document is the single source of truth for what every AI-assistant
4	vendor integration must provide. A new connector is a set of files
5	implementing the protocols listed here, plus a registry registration. No
6	shared subsystem (logging, branding, scheduler, AppDelegate) needs to be
7	edited per vendor — the registry is the only seam.
8	
9	The contract is compile-time. There is no runtime plugin loader, no
10	external dylib, no dynamic discovery. Adding a vendor is a code change
11	that ships with the binary.
12	
13	This contract composes with — and never relaxes — the Swift quality docs:
14	`SWIFT-CONCURRENCY.md`, `SWIFT-ERROR-HANDLING.md`, `SWIFT-IO-ROBUSTNESS.md`,
15	`SWIFT-TESTABILITY.md`, `SWIFT-VALUE-OBJECTS.md`. Read those first; this
16	contract assumes them.
17	
18	## 1. Plugin points
19	
20	A vendor integration declares one `VendorBundle` value type that bundles
21	every plugin point. The bundle is the only public surface the registry
22	reads.
23	
24	| Plugin point             | Type / protocol             | Required? |
25	|--------------------------|-----------------------------|-----------|
26	| Vendor identity          | `Vendor` (string-backed)    | yes       |
27	| Branding                 | `VendorBranding`            | yes       |
28	| Usage fetching           | `any UsageConnector`        | yes       |
29	| Credential locator       | `any CredentialLocator`     | yes (driven by the connector) |
30	| Payload sanitizer        | `any PayloadSanitizing`     | yes       |
31	| Logger                   | `FileLogger`                | yes       |
32	| Logging proxy            | `LoggingProxy`              | yes (composed from logger + sanitizer) |
33	| Status / outages         | `any StatusConnector`       | optional  |
34	| Active-account monitor   | `any ActiveAccountMonitoring` | optional |
35	| Documentation pointer    | `VendorDocumentation`       | yes       |
36	
37	`status` and `activeAccountMonitor` are optional because a vendor may not
38	expose either — the framework treats `nil` as "feature disabled for this
39	vendor", not "partial implementation".
40	
41	## 2. Vendor identity
42	
43	`Vendor` is a string-backed value type defined in the lib's `Models/`. New
44	vendors add a `static let` next to the existing ones. The string is the
45	primary key of the vendor across persisted JSON, so it is forward-compatible:
46	unknown vendors decode without throwing.
47	
48	A vendor identifier MUST be lowercase, ASCII, and stable for the lifetime of
49	the connector — renaming it breaks history files written by older builds.
50	
51	## 3. Branding
52	
53	`VendorBranding` is a value type with these fields:
54	
55	| Field         | Type    | Notes                                       |
56	|---------------|---------|---------------------------------------------|
57	| `vendor`      | `Vendor`| identity                                    |
58	| `displayName` | `String`| human-readable name shown in the UI         |
59	| `tintHex`     | `String`| 6-digit hex tint applied to the menu bar icon |
60	| `assetName`   | `String`| basename of the PDF mark in `App/Resources/VendorBranding/` (vector, scales without quality loss) |
61	
62	Each vendor ships its mark as a `<assetName>.pdf` placed under
63	`App/Resources/VendorBranding/`. Vector PDF is mandatory because the menu
64	bar renders the icon at multiple sizes. The conversion-from-SVG flow lives
65	under `scripts/render-<vendor>-mark.swift`.
66	
67	The registry — not a switch statement — resolves a `Vendor` to its
68	`VendorBranding`. Unknown vendors return `nil` and the UI falls back to a
69	text-only label.
70	
71	## 4. Usage connector
72	
73	```swift
74	public protocol UsageConnector: Sendable {
75	    var vendor: Vendor { get }
76	    func fetchUsages() async throws -> [VendorUsageEntry]
77	    func resolveActiveAccount() -> AccountEmail?
78	}
79	```
80	
81	Invariants:
82	
83	- Connectors are `actor`s. `vendor` is `nonisolated` so the poller can read
84	  it without hopping isolation.
85	- `resolveActiveAccount()` must be cheap and synchronous-ish — it runs on
86	  every poll tick and on every menu refresh.
87	- `fetchUsages()` must never throw past the boundary on a recoverable
88	  error. It returns an entry with `lastError` set instead so the previous
89	  metrics stay visible.
90	- Every emitted `UsageMetric` must be `.timeWindow` or `.payAsYouGo` —
91	  never `.unknown`. The contract conformance test enforces this.
92	- Every emitted `resetAt` must parse as a strict ISO 8601 datetime via
93	  `ISODate.parsing(_:)`. Calendar dates (e.g. a vendor field handed back
94	  as `yyyy-MM-dd`) are promoted to UTC midnight via
95	  `ISODate.parsingFlexibleDate(_:)` at the connector boundary.
96	- The connector emits whatever number of metrics the vendor exposes — no
97	  artificial cap, no "hide if zero" filter. The UI decides what to render.
98	
99	## 5. Credential locator
100	
101	The application **never** owns, refreshes, rotates, or persists tokens.
102	Each vendor's CLI (`claude`, `codex`, `gh`) keeps full lifecycle ownership.
103	The locator's job is purely to **read** what the vendor's CLI has already
104	written.
105	
106	```swift
107	public protocol CredentialLocator: Sendable {
108	    associatedtype Credentials: Sendable
109	    /// Reads credentials from external sources owned by the vendor's CLI.
110	    /// MUST NOT write, refresh, rotate, or persist tokens.
111	    func locate() async throws -> Credentials
112	}
113	```
114	
115	The associated `Credentials` type lets each vendor carry its own
116	domain-specific shape (OAuth token vs API key + org id vs token + login)
117	without lossy generalization.
118	
119	### Read-only invariant
120	
121	Locators MUST NOT:
122	
123	- call `SecItemAdd`, `SecItemUpdate`, `SecItemDelete`;
124	- write to the vendor's config files;
125	- shell out to `<vendor> auth login`, `<vendor> auth logout`, or any
126	  credential-mutating subcommand.
127	
128	A SwiftLint custom rule flags writes inside any `*CredentialLocator.swift`
129	file at build time.
130	
131	### Injectability
132	
133	Every external dependency is constructor-injected:
134	
135	- `environment: [String: String]`
136	- `fileManager: FileManager`
137	- `processRunner: ProcessRunning`
138	- `keychainAccessor: KeychainReading` (when applicable)
139	
140	No locator may call `ProcessInfo.processInfo`, `SecKeychain*`, or
141	`/usr/bin/security` directly — every such call goes through an injectable
142	collaborator.
143	
144	### Error contract
145	
146	Errors are enum cases with associated values per `SWIFT-ERROR-HANDLING.md`.
147	Two error classes are mandatory and distinct:
148	
149	- "not logged in" — the vendor's CLI never wrote credentials. Normal user
150	  state, surfaced as a calm message.
151	- "found but unreadable" — file or keychain entry exists but is
152	  malformed. Bug or vendor schema change, surfaced as an actionable
153	  diagnostic.
154	
155	## 6. Payload sanitization
156	
157	Every payload (request body, response body, headers, error message)
158	emitted to a log MUST go through a vendor-specific sanitizer **before**
159	reaching the file. Sanitization is enforced at the logger boundary, not
160	at the call site.
161	
162	```swift
163	public protocol PayloadSanitizing: Sendable {
164	    /// Returns a copy of `payload` with confidential fields stripped or masked.
165	    /// MUST be idempotent and side-effect free.
166	    func sanitize(_ payload: Data) -> Data
167	    func sanitize(_ headers: [String: String]) -> [String: String]
168	    func sanitize(_ message: String) -> String
169	}
170	```
171	
172	### Default-deny header rules
173	
174	Composed into every per-vendor sanitizer via `BaseHeaderSanitizer`:
175	
176	| Header (case-insensitive) | Replacement   |
177	|---------------------------|---------------|
178	| `Authorization`           | `<redacted>`  |
179	| `Cookie`, `Set-Cookie`    | `<redacted>`  |
180	| `X-Api-Key`               | `<redacted>`  |
181	| `X-Auth-Token`            | `<redacted>`  |
182	| `Proxy-Authorization`     | `<redacted>`  |
183	
184	### Email-pattern rule
185	
186	A regex `[A-Za-z0-9._+-]+@[A-Za-z0-9.-]+` matches email-like strings in
187	bodies and messages and replaces them with `<email>`. The vendor doc's
188	`Sanitized fields` section lists any field where this default must be
189	suppressed (rare — only when the vendor publishes the field as part of
190	its public API surface).
191	
192	### Per-vendor field list
193	
194	Every connector's `docs/vendors/<vendor>.md` carries an exhaustive
195	`Sanitized fields` section. That list drives the leakage test (see §8) and
196	is the contract: a field added upstream that isn't on the list is by
197	default a sanitization gap.
198	
199	### LoggingProxy
200	
201	`LoggingProxy` wraps a `FileLogger` and a `PayloadSanitizing`. Connectors
202	log payload-bearing entries through the proxy only — no direct
203	`logger.log(.debug, "...payload...")` allowed. A SwiftLint custom rule
204	enforces this at build time on `*Connector.swift` and
205	`*CredentialLocator.swift` files.
206	
207	Proxy entry format (one log line per HTTP exchange):
208	
209	```
210	<method> <url> -> HTTP <status> in <latency>ms
211	  request headers: <sanitized headers JSON>
212	  request body: <sanitized body or "<empty>">
213	  response headers: <sanitized headers JSON>
214	  response body: <sanitized body or "<truncated, N bytes>">
215	```
216	
217	Deterministic line shape because testers attach raw log files; structured
218	lines make triage feasible.
219	
220	## 7. Status connector
221	
222	```swift
223	public protocol StatusConnector: Sendable {
224	    var vendor: Vendor { get }
225	    func fetchOutages() async throws -> [Outage]
226	}
227	```
228	
229	Optional. A vendor without a public statuspage simply omits this slot —
230	the registry threads `nil` through to the poller, which skips status calls
231	for that vendor.
232	
233	## 8. Active-account monitor
234	
235	```swift
236	public protocol ActiveAccountMonitoring: Sendable {
237	    var vendor: Vendor { get }
238	    func start() async
239	    func stop() async
240	}
241	```
242	
243	Optional. A monitor watches the local source the vendor's CLI writes
244	(JSON config, YAML hosts file, keychain entry, …) and fires a callback
245	when the active identity changes. The callback signature stays
246	vendor-specific — the protocol only formalizes the lifecycle so the
247	registry can drive `start()` / `stop()` uniformly.
248	
249	`start()` must be idempotent — calling it twice is a no-op.
250	
251	## 9. Logger registration
252	
253	Every `VendorBundle` carries its own `FileLogger`. The default file path
254	is `~/.cache/ai-usages-tracker/<vendor>-usages-connector.log`. Adding a
255	vendor does not require editing `Loggers` or any cross-cutting subsystem;
256	the bundle owns the logger declaration.
257	
258	`LogCleaner` discovers loggers via the registry, so retention applies
259	automatically to any newly added vendor.
260	
261	## 10. Verbose vendor mode
262	
263	The framework supports a debug mode where a single vendor's connector
264	emits, at `.debug` level, the **sanitized** payloads of every API
265	exchange. Production builds with neither activation source set
266	**never** ship verbose logging — it requires either an explicit
267	environment variable or a build-time Info.plist key.
268	
269	Resolution order, first match wins:
270	
271	1. Environment variable `AI_TRACKER_VENDOR_DEBUG=<vendor-slug>` —
272	   power-user override on standard builds.
273	2. Info.plist key `AITrackerVendorDebug` — set by nightly tester builds
274	   attached to a `type:new-assistant` PR. Scoped to the vendor under test.
275	
276	The wiring layer reads this at startup, identifies "the verbose vendor",
277	and constructs that vendor's `LoggingProxy` at `.debug` level. All other
278	vendors retain their normal proxy. Bundle declarations are unaware of the
279	setting.
280	
281	## 11. Vendor documentation
282	
283	Every vendor ships a `docs/vendors/<vendor>.md` whose template is
284	`docs/vendors/_TEMPLATE.md`. The doc is treated as a **dated snapshot** of
285	how the vendor's API behaves at a given moment, not as a forever-true
286	reference — vendor APIs drift silently, and the historical baseline is
287	the only artifact that lets a future contributor compare what the API
288	used to look like against what it does now.
289	
290	Mandatory sections (the template enforces order and headings):
291	
292	- **Last verified header.** A machine-parseable `> **Last verified:**
293	  YYYY-MM-DD by @handle on <plan>` directly under the H1.
294	- **Endpoints.** Full URL, method, headers, timeout, response
295	  content-type. Each entry dated (`_verified: YYYY-MM-DD_`).
296	- **Credential sources.** Cascade order, exact paths and keys, names of
297	  the vendor CLIs that own each source. Dated.
298	- **Sanitized fields.** Exhaustive list of fields stripped from logs:
299	  tokens, keys, refresh tokens, raw cookies, secret account ids,
300	  email-like patterns. Each entry includes its location in the payload
301	  and the redaction style. This list drives the leakage test. **A field
302	  added upstream that isn't on this list is by default a sanitization
303	  gap.**
304	- **Plan variants observed.** Free / Pro / Team / Enterprise — each with a
305	  representative dated sanitized payload and the fields the connector
306	  reads. Variants assumed but not yet **verified by a tester** are marked
307	  explicitly as such.
308	- **Metric semantics.** Per `UsageMetric` emitted by the connector,
309	  mapping from raw payload fields to the Swift case. Includes reset
310	  cadence (rolling vs calendar boundary), unit, edge cases (free-tier
311	  overage, "unlimited" flags). Dated.
312	- **Time semantics.** Whether the API returns ISO 8601 datetimes or
313	  calendar dates, and whether the connector promotes calendar dates to
314	  UTC midnight.
315	- **Error catalog.** HTTP status codes seen in the wild and the
316	  connector's response (degrade, retry, surface). Dated.
317	- **Known unknowns.** Behaviors the contributor could not verify directly
318	  (e.g. enterprise plans without a tester). Filled in over time by the
319	  tester gate workflow.
320	- **Source references.** Community write-ups, official docs — every link
321	  followed by `(retrieved YYYY-MM-DD)`.
322	- **Change log.** Append-only `YYYY-MM-DD — what changed`. The first entry
323	  is "initial capture". Subsequent entries fill in as drift is discovered.
324	
325	### Sample payload format
326	
327	Captured payloads are fenced JSON blocks prefixed with a comment:
328	
329	```markdown
330	<!-- captured: 2026-05-07, plan: Pro, login: redacted -->
331	```
332	
333	Sensitive fields are redacted but the structure, types, and field
334	presence are preserved. Multiple snapshots over time can coexist — older
335	ones get a `(superseded by YYYY-MM-DD)` annotation rather than being
336	deleted, so the drift trail is visible.
337	
338	### Re-verification discipline
339	
340	The reviewer checklist for any PR touching a connector requires bumping
341	`Last verified` and appending a Change log entry. A stale doc is a

file.content:
# Vendor plugin contract

This document is the single source of truth for what every AI-assistant
vendor integration must provide. A new connector is a set of files
implementing the protocols listed here, plus a registry registration. No
shared subsystem (logging, branding, scheduler, AppDelegate) needs to be
edited per vendor — the registry is the only seam.

The contract is compile-time. There is no runtime plugin loader, no
external dylib, no dynamic discovery. Adding a vendor is a code change
that ships with the binary.

This contract composes with — and never relaxes — the Swift quality docs:
`SWIFT-CONCURRENCY.md`, `SWIFT-ERROR-HANDLING.md`, `SWIFT-IO-ROBUSTNESS.md`,
`SWIFT-TESTABILITY.md`, `SWIFT-VALUE-OBJECTS.md`. Read those first; this
contract assumes them.

## 1. Plugin points

A vendor integration declares one `VendorBundle` value type that bundles
every plugin point. The bundle is the only public surface the registry
reads.

| Plugin point             | Type / protocol             | Required? |
|--------------------------|-----------------------------|-----------|
| Vendor identity          | `Vendor` (string-backed)    | yes       |
| Branding                 | `VendorBranding`            | yes       |
| Usage fetching           | `any UsageConnector`        | yes       |
| Credential locator       | `any CredentialLocator`     | yes (driven by the connector) |
| Payload sanitizer        | `any PayloadSanitizing`     | yes       |
| Logger                   | `FileLogger`                | yes       |
| Logging proxy            | `LoggingProxy`              | yes (composed from logger + sanitizer) |
| Status / outages         | `any StatusConnector`       | optional  |
| Active-account monitor   | `any ActiveAccountMonitoring` | optional |
| Documentation pointer    | `VendorDocumentation`       | yes       |

`status` and `activeAccountMonitor` are optional because a vendor may not
expose either — the framework treats `nil` as "feature disabled for this
vendor", not "partial implementation".

## 2. Vendor identity

`Vendor` is a string-backed value type defined in the lib's `Models/`. New
vendors add a `static let` next to the existing ones. The string is the
primary key of the vendor across persisted JSON, so it is forward-compatible:
unknown vendors decode without throwing.

A vendor identifier MUST be lowercase, ASCII, and stable for the lifetime of
the connector — renaming it breaks history files written by older builds.

## 3. Branding

`VendorBranding` is a value type with these fields:

| Field         | Type    | Notes                                       |
|---------------|---------|---------------------------------------------|
| `vendor`      | `Vendor`| identity                                    |
| `displayName` | `String`| human-readable name shown in the UI         |
| `tintHex`     | `String`| 6-digit hex tint applied to the menu bar icon |
| `assetName`   | `String`| basename of the PDF mark in `App/Resources/VendorBranding/` (vector, scales without quality loss) |

Each vendor ships its mark as a `<assetName>.pdf` placed under
`App/Resources/VendorBranding/`. Vector PDF is mandatory because the menu
bar renders the icon at multiple sizes. The conversion-from-SVG flow lives
under `scripts/render-<vendor>-mark.swift`.

The registry — not a switch statement — resolves a `Vendor` to its
`VendorBranding`. Unknown vendors return `nil` and the UI falls back to a
text-only label.

## 4. Usage connector

```swift
public protocol UsageConnector: Sendable {
    var vendor: Vendor { get }
    func fetchUsages() async throws -> [VendorUsageEntry]
    func resolveActiveAccount() -> AccountEmail?
}
```

Invariants:

- Connectors are `actor`s. `vendor` is `nonisolated` so the poller can read
  it without hopping isolation.
- `resolveActiveAccount()` must be cheap and synchronous-ish — it runs on
  every poll tick and on every menu refresh.
- `fetchUsages()` must never throw past the boundary on a recoverable
  error. It returns an entry with `lastError` set instead so the previous
  metrics stay visible.
- Every emitted `UsageMetric` must be `.timeWindow` or `.payAsYouGo` —
  never `.unknown`. The contract conformance test enforces this.
- Every emitted `resetAt` must parse as a strict ISO 8601 datetime via
  `ISODate.parsing(_:)`. Calendar dates (e.g. a vendor field handed back
  as `yyyy-MM-dd`) are promoted to UTC midnight via
  `ISODate.parsingFlexibleDate(_:)` at the connector boundary.
- The connector emits whatever number of metrics the vendor exposes — no
  artificial cap, no "hide if zero" filter. The UI decides what to render.

## 5. Credential locator

The application **never** owns, refreshes, rotates, or persists tokens.
Each vendor's CLI (`claude`, `codex`, `gh`) keeps full lifecycle ownership.
The locator's job is purely to **read** what the vendor's CLI has already
written.

```swift
public protocol CredentialLocator: Sendable {
    associatedtype Credentials: Sendable
    /// Reads credentials from external sources owned by the vendor's CLI.
    /// MUST NOT write, refresh, rotate, or persist tokens.
    func locate() async throws -> Credentials
}
```

The associated `Credentials` type lets each vendor carry its own
domain-specific shape (OAuth token vs API key + org id vs token + login)
without lossy generalization.

### Read-only invariant

Locators MUST NOT:

- call `SecItemAdd`, `SecItemUpdate`, `SecItemDelete`;
- write to the vendor's config files;
- shell out to `<vendor> auth login`, `<vendor> auth logout`, or any
  credential-mutating subcommand.

A SwiftLint custom rule flags writes inside any `*CredentialLocator.swift`
file at build time.

### Injectability

Every external dependency is constructor-injected:

- `environment: [String: String]`
- `fileManager: FileManager`
- `processRunner: ProcessRunning`
- `keychainAccessor: KeychainReading` (when applicable)

No locator may call `ProcessInfo.processInfo`, `SecKeychain*`, or
`/usr/bin/security` directly — every such call goes through an injectable
collaborator.

### Error contract

Errors are enum cases with associated values per `SWIFT-ERROR-HANDLING.md`.
Two error classes are mandatory and distinct:

- "not logged in" — the vendor's CLI never wrote credentials. Normal user
  state, surfaced as a calm message.
- "found but unreadable" — file or keychain entry exists but is
  malformed. Bug or vendor schema change, surfaced as an actionable
  diagnostic.

## 6. Payload sanitization

Every payload (request body, response body, headers, error message)
emitted to a log MUST go through a vendor-specific sanitizer **before**
reaching the file. Sanitization is enforced at the logger boundary, not
at the call site.

```swift
public protocol PayloadSanitizing: Sendable {
    /// Returns a copy of `payload` with confidential fields stripped or masked.
    /// MUST be idempotent and side-effect free.
    func sanitize(_ payload: Data) -> Data
    func sanitize(_ headers: [String: String]) -> [String: String]
    func sanitize(_ message: String) -> String
}
```

### Default-deny header rules

Composed into every per-vendor sanitizer via `BaseHeaderSanitizer`:

| Header (case-insensitive) | Replacement   |
|---------------------------|---------------|
| `Authorization`           | `<redacted>`  |
| `Cookie`, `Set-Cookie`    | `<redacted>`  |
| `X-Api-Key`               | `<redacted>`  |
| `X-Auth-Token`            | `<redacted>`  |
| `Proxy-Authorization`     | `<redacted>`  |

### Email-pattern rule

A regex `[A-Za-z0-9._+-]+@[A-Za-z0-9.-]+` matches email-like strings in
bodies and messages and replaces them with `<email>`. The vendor doc's
`Sanitized fields` section lists any field where this default must be
suppressed (rare — only when the vendor publishes the field as part of
its public API surface).

### Per-vendor field list

Every connector's `docs/vendors/<vendor>.md` carries an exhaustive
`Sanitized fields` section. That list drives the leakage test (see §8) and
is the contract: a field added upstream that isn't on the list is by
default a sanitization gap.

### LoggingProxy

`LoggingProxy` wraps a `FileLogger` and a `PayloadSanitizing`. Connectors
log payload-bearing entries through the proxy only — no direct
`logger.log(.debug, "...payload...")` allowed. A SwiftLint custom rule
enforces this at build time on `*Connector.swift` and
`*CredentialLocator.swift` files.

Proxy entry format (one log line per HTTP exchange):

```
<method> <url> -> HTTP <status> in <latency>ms
  request headers: <sanitized headers JSON>
  request body: <sanitized body or "<empty>">
  response headers: <sanitized headers JSON>
  response body: <sanitized body or "<truncated, N bytes>">
```

Deterministic line shape because testers attach raw log files; structured
lines make triage feasible.

## 7. Status connector

```swift
public protocol StatusConnector: Sendable {
    var vendor: Vendor { get }
    func fetchOutages() async throws -> [Outage]
}
```

Optional. A vendor without a public statuspage simply omits this slot —
the registry threads `nil` through to the poller, which skips status calls
for that vendor.

## 8. Active-account monitor

```swift
public protocol ActiveAccountMonitoring: Sendable {
    var vendor: Vendor { get }
    func start() async
    func stop() async
}
```

Optional. A monitor watches the local source the vendor's CLI writes
(JSON config, YAML hosts file, keychain entry, …) and fires a callback
when the active identity changes. The callback signature stays
vendor-specific — the protocol only formalizes the lifecycle so the
registry can drive `start()` / `stop()` uniformly.

`start()` must be idempotent — calling it twice is a no-op.

## 9. Logger registration

Every `VendorBundle` carries its own `FileLogger`. The default file path
is `~/.cache/ai-usages-tracker/<vendor>-usages-connector.log`. Adding a
vendor does not require editing `Loggers` or any cross-cutting subsystem;
the bundle owns the logger declaration.

`LogCleaner` discovers loggers via the registry, so retention applies
automatically to any newly added vendor.

## 10. Verbose vendor mode

The framework supports a debug mode where a single vendor's connector
emits, at `.debug` level, the **sanitized** payloads of every API
exchange. Production builds with neither activation source set
**never** ship verbose logging — it requires either an explicit
environment variable or a build-time Info.plist key.

Resolution order, first match wins:

1. Environment variable `AI_TRACKER_VENDOR_DEBUG=<vendor-slug>` —
   power-user override on standard builds.
2. Info.plist key `AITrackerVendorDebug` — set by nightly tester builds
   attached to a `type:new-assistant` PR. Scoped to the vendor under test.

The wiring layer reads this at startup, identifies "the verbose vendor",
and constructs that vendor's `LoggingProxy` at `.debug` level. All other
vendors retain their normal proxy. Bundle declarations are unaware of the
setting.

## 11. Vendor documentation

Every vendor ships a `docs/vendors/<vendor>.md` whose template is
`docs/vendors/_TEMPLATE.md`. The doc is treated as a **dated snapshot** of
how the vendor's API behaves at a given moment, not as a forever-true
reference — vendor APIs drift silently, and the historical baseline is
the only artifact that lets a future contributor compare what the API
used to look like against what it does now.

Mandatory sections (the template enforces order and headings):

- **Last verified header.** A machine-parseable `> **Last verified:**
  YYYY-MM-DD by @handle on <plan>` directly under the H1.
- **Endpoints.** Full URL, method, headers, timeout, response
  content-type. Each entry dated (`_verified: YYYY-MM-DD_`).
- **Credential sources.** Cascade order, exact paths and keys, names of
  the vendor CLIs that own each source. Dated.
- **Sanitized fields.** Exhaustive list of fields stripped from logs:
  tokens, keys, refresh tokens, raw cookies, secret account ids,
  email-like patterns. Each entry includes its location in the payload
  and the redaction style. This list drives the leakage test. **A field
  added upstream that isn't on this list is by default a sanitization
  gap.**
- **Plan variants observed.** Free / Pro / Team / Enterprise — each with a
  representative dated sanitized payload and the fields the connector
  reads. Variants assumed but not yet **verified by a tester** are marked
  explicitly as such.
- **Metric semantics.** Per `UsageMetric` emitted by the connector,
  mapping from raw payload fields to the Swift case. Includes reset
  cadence (rolling vs calendar boundary), unit, edge cases (free-tier
  overage, "unlimited" flags). Dated.
- **Time semantics.** Whether the API returns ISO 8601 datetimes or
  calendar dates, and whether the connector promotes calendar dates to
  UTC midnight.
- **Error catalog.** HTTP status codes seen in the wild and the
  connector's response (degrade, retry, surface). Dated.
- **Known unknowns.** Behaviors the contributor could not verify directly
  (e.g. enterprise plans without a tester). Filled in over time by the
  tester gate workflow.
- **Source references.** Community write-ups, official docs — every link
  followed by `(retrieved YYYY-MM-DD)`.
- **Change log.** Append-only `YYYY-MM-DD — what changed`. The first entry
  is "initial capture". Subsequent entries fill in as drift is discovered.

### Sample payload format

Captured payloads are fenced JSON blocks prefixed with a comment:

```markdown
<!-- captured: 2026-05-07, plan: Pro, login: redacted -->
```

Sensitive fields are redacted but the structure, types, and field
presence are preserved. Multiple snapshots over time can coexist — older
ones get a `(superseded by YYYY-MM-DD)` annotation rather than being
deleted, so the drift trail is visible.

### Re-verification discipline

The reviewer checklist for any PR touching a connector requires bumping
`Last verified` and appending a Change log entry. A stale doc is a
contract violation.

## 12. Registry and AppDelegate

```swift
public enum VendorRegistry {
    public static let bundles: [VendorBundle] = [
        ClaudeCodePlugin.bundle,
        CodexPlugin.bundle,
    ]
}
```

`AppDelegate` composes assistants from `VendorRegistry.bundles` only — no
named per-vendor properties. Adding a vendor means adding one line to the
registry; nothing else above the registry edits.

## 13. Conformance test

A single `VendorRegistryConformanceTests` walks `VendorRegistry.bundles`
and asserts:

- Every emitted `MetricKind` is `.timeWindow` or `.payAsYouGo` (no
  `.unknown`).
- Every `resetAt` parses as a strict ISO 8601 datetime via
  `ISODate.parsing(_:)`.
- Every branding entry resolves to an existing PDF asset under
  `App/Resources/VendorBranding/`.
- Every `documentation` pointer points at an existing
  `docs/vendors/<vendor>.md` carrying a parseable `Last verified:` line.
- The leakage test fixture for each vendor exists and produces no
  surviving secret after sanitization.

## 14. Onboarding scaffolding

Scaffolding is **skill-only** — there is no shell script. The skill
defined in the `new-assistant-onboarding-workflow` epic creates every
required file directly via `Write` / `Edit`, using this contract as its
template source. This contract specifies the shape; the skill specifies
the procedure.
